item.title: The Stack
item.description: Candidate UK-owned vendors across the technology stack — working draft, internal review only.

item.title: What's Next
item.description: Open questions and provocations for discussion.

item.title: About
item.description: About the Sovereign Tech Stack initiative — who is building it, and what comes next.

item.title: Join Us
item.description: Current openings at Sovereign Tech Stack — founding roles for the incubation phase and commercial launch.

item.title: Leadership Team
item.description: The Sovereign Tech Stack leadership team — being assembled now for the incubation phase and commercial launch.

item.title: The Case
item.description: Why UK digital sovereignty matters — and why this is the moment to build.

item.title: The Market
item.description: The addressable market for UK-owned technology certification — buyers, scale, and revenue model.

item.title: The Platform
item.description: How the Sovereign Tech Stack certification platform works — standards, structure, and commercial model.

item.title: Building a British Tech Ecosystem
item.description: The UK has the vendors. What it lacks is the connective tissue that turns a collection of products into a coherent alternative.

item.title: Why Data Residency Is No Longer Optional
item.description: Data residency has moved from a nice-to-have to a procurement requirement — and the trajectory is only going in one direction.

item.title: What the Sovereign AI Fund Means for British SMEs
item.description: The UK government has committed £500 million to UK AI — here is what it means in practice for small businesses.

item.title: The Case for UK-Owned Infrastructure
item.description: Most British businesses have never questioned their technology stack. They should.
